svg.title: Code scanner system architecture
svg.desc: POST /scan flows from browser through Lambda A dispatcher, S3 and SQS, Lambda B, then to Bandit/Semgrep for small files or ECS Fargate for large files, writing results to S3 and DynamoDB. GET /status returns a presigned S3 URL. GET /history returns last 50 scans.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Lambda A validate and dispatch POST /scan requests?')
sendPrompt('What gets stored in S3 when code is submitted for scanning?')
sendPrompt('Why is SQS used between Lambda A and Lambda B?')
sendPrompt('How does Lambda B decide between Bandit/Semgrep and ECS Fargate?')
sendPrompt('What vulnerabilities do Bandit and Semgrep find?')
sendPrompt('Why use ECS Fargate for large file scanning instead of Lambda?')
sendPrompt('What does the S3 scan report JSON file contain?')
sendPrompt('What scan metadata gets written to DynamoDB ScanResults?')
sendPrompt('How does Lambda A generate a presigned S3 URL for GET /status?')
sendPrompt('How does Lambda A fetch the last 50 scans for GET /history?')
